title: Multi-Sensor Environmental Baseline & Drift Detection
dataSources: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=temperature OR metric_name=humidity OR metric_name=pressure OR metric_name=light, `sourcetype=edge_hub`
app: Splunk Edge Hub (multi-sensor fusion), Splunk OT Intelligence
spl:
| mstats avg(temperature) as temp, avg(humidity) as rh, avg(pressure) as press, avg(light_level) as lux by host, _time span=1h
| stats avg(temp) as avg_temp, stdev(temp) as std_temp,
        avg(rh) as avg_rh, stdev(rh) as std_rh,
        avg(press) as avg_press, stdev(press) as std_press,
        avg(lux) as avg_lux, stdev(lux) as std_lux by host
| eval temp_anomaly=if(std_temp > 5, "DRIFT", "NORMAL"),
        rh_anomaly=if(std_rh > 15, "DRIFT", "NORMAL"),
        press_anomaly=if(std_press > 10, "DRIFT", "NORMAL"),
        lux_anomaly=if(std_lux > 5000, "DRIFT", "NORMAL")
| where temp_anomaly="DRIFT" OR rh_anomaly="DRIFT" OR press_anomaly="DRIFT" OR lux_anomaly="DRIFT"
description: Detects sensor failures, calibration drift, or environmental changes by correlating expected relationships between temperature, humidity, pressure, and light.
value: Detects sensor failures, calibration drift, or environmental changes by correlating expected relationships between temperature, humidity, pressure, and light.
implementation: Enable all available sensors on Edge Hub (temperature, humidity, optional pressure, optional light). Configure 1-hour aggregation interval (Advanced Settings → Sensor Polling). Establish 30-day baseline for expected correlation between sensors (e.g., temp and humidity should not fluctuate independently in sealed rooms). Use MLTK Smart Outlier Detection to detect when sensor relationships break down (indicator of sensor failure or environmental change). Store baseline profiles in edge-hub-data index for historical comparison.
visualization: Multi-sensor correlation matrix, drift detection alerts, baseline comparison chart.
knownFalsePositives: Compliance breaches during planned excursions for cleaning, filter changes, or controlled commissioning.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (multi-sensor fusion), Splunk OT Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=temperature OR metric_name=humidity OR metric_name=pressure OR metric_name=light, `sourcetype=edge_hub`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable all available sensors on Edge Hub (temperature, humidity, optional pressure, optional light). Configure 1-hour aggregation interval (Advanced Settings → Sensor Polling). Establish 30-day baseline for expected correlation between sensors (e.g., temp and humidity should not fluctuate independently in sealed rooms). Use MLTK Smart Outlier Detection to detect when sensor relationships break down (indicator of sensor failure or environmental change). Store baseline profiles in edge-hub-data in…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| mstats avg(temperature) as temp, avg(humidity) as rh, avg(pressure) as press, avg(light_level) as lux by host, _time span=1h
| stats avg(temp) as avg_temp, stdev(temp) as std_temp,
        avg(rh) as avg_rh, stdev(rh) as std_rh,
        avg(press) as avg_press, stdev(press) as std_press,
        avg(lux) as avg_lux, stdev(lux) as std_lux by host
| eval temp_anomaly=if(std_temp > 5, "DRIFT", "NORMAL"),
        rh_anomaly=if(std_rh > 15, "DRIFT", "NORMAL"),
        press_anomaly=if(std_press > 10, "DRIFT", "NORMAL"),
        lux_anomaly=if(std_lux > 5000, "DRIFT", "NORMAL")
| where temp_anomaly="DRIFT" OR rh_anomaly="DRIFT" OR press_anomaly="DRIFT" OR lux_anomaly="DRIFT"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Multi-Sensor Environmental Baseline & Drift Detection** — Detects sensor failures, calibration drift, or environmental changes by correlating expected relationships between temperature, humidity, pressure, and light.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=temperature OR metric_name=humidity OR metric_name=pressure OR metric_name=light, `sourcetype=edge_hub`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (multi-sensor fusion), Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `mstats` to query metrics indexes (pre-aggregated metric data).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **temp_anomaly** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where temp_anomaly="DRIFT" OR rh_anomaly="DRIFT" OR press_anomaly="DRIFT" OR lux_anomaly="DRIFT"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Note: for mstats, confirm the metrics indexes in the search exist and your role can run mstats. This is separate from CIM data model acceleration.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Multi-sensor correlation matrix, drift detection alerts, baseline comparison chart.
grandmaExplanation: We watch what edge sensors report from the physical world. We help you know if something is off with this area: multi-sensor environmental baseline & drift before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for CIM; ensure metrics index access and mstats permissions for the indexes in the search.
